segments.text: It is for freedom that Christ has set us free.
Stand firm, then, and do not let yourselves be burdened again by a yoke of slavery.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Mark my words! If you let yourselves be circumcised, Christ will be of no value to you at all.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Again I declare to every man who lets himself be circumcised that he is obligated to obey the whole law.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: You who are trying to be justified by the law have been alienated from Christ; you have fallen away from grace.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For through the Spirit we eagerly await by faith the righteousness for which we hope.
For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision has any value. The only thing that counts is faith expressing itself through love.
You were running a good race. Who cut in on you to keep you from obeying the truth?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: That kind of persuasion does not come from the one who calls you.
A little yeast works through the whole batch of dough.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I am confident in the Lord that you will take no other view. The one who is throwing you into confusion, whoever that may be, will have to pay the penalty.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Brothers and sisters, if I am still preaching circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? In that case the offense of the cross has been abolished.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: As for those agitators, I wish they would go the whole way and emasculate themselves!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: You, my brothers and sisters, were called to be free. But do not use your freedom to indulge the flesh; rather, serve one another humbly in love.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For the entire law is fulfilled in keeping this one command:
Love your neighbor as yourself.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you bite and devour each other, watch out or you will be destroyed by each other.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: So I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For the flesh desires what is contrary to the Spirit, and the Spirit what is contrary to the flesh. They are in conflict with each other, so that you are not to do whatever you want.
But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
The acts of the flesh are obvious: sexual immorality, impurity and debauchery;
idolatry and witchcraft; hatred, discord, jealousy, fits of rage, selfish ambition, dissensions, factions
and envy; drunkenness, orgies, and the like.
I warn you, as I did before, that those who live like this will not inherit the kingdom of God.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
gentleness and self-control. Against such things there is no law.
Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
Since we live by the Spirit, let us keep in step with the Spirit.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Let us not become conceited, provoking and envying each other.
segments.speaker: Paul
